PLAINTIFF 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against East Bay Construction & Management Llc [Code Civ. Proc. 2023.030].

Plaintiff 2075 Sutter Street Homeowners Association's motion for terminating sanctions against defendant East Bay Construction & Management, LLC is granted. The answer filed by East Bay Construction on December 18, 2023 is stricken and the default of East Bay Construction is entered. No opposition was filed to this motion. With the trial date imminent, no less drastic sanction will remediate the prejudice suffered by the Homeowners Association resulting from the combination of East Bay Construction's failure to comply with the order compelling its PMK deposition, its failure to seek relief from its suspended corporate status, and its failure to be represented by counsel after the order allowing its prior counsel to withdraw.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff 2075 Sutter Street Homeowners Association's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted conditioned on its obtaining a continuance of the trial date to avoid prejudice to newly named defendants. The motion is unopposed.

Plaintiff seeks to amend the complaint to state the amount of damages sought. The court finds no prejudice to defendant as the amount is based on repair and reconstruction estimates that were disclosed in expert discovery. (Kim Dec. paras. 15 & 16 & Ex. 3.) This proposed amendment is granted unconditionally.

Plaintiff further seeks to amend the complaint to substitute Leonardo Paredes and Emperador Construction, Inc. as Does 1 and 2. The court's concern with this amendment is the proximity to the trial date of May 12. Plaintiff's moving papers establish that it has not unduly delayed naming these defendants; defendant East Bay Construction & Management, LLC (EBCM) was not forthcoming in discovery and Plaintiff did not identify these defendants until recently. (Kim Dec. paras. 9-12.) Nonetheless, a substitution less than a month before trial does not give these defendants time to answer, conduct discovery, or prepare for trial. Accordingly, Plaintiff's motion to substitute these defendants for Does 1 and 2 is conditionally granted on Plaintiff's obtaining a trial continuance, with discovery reopened, of at least six months in Department 206, the master calendar department with authority to set trial dates. Plaintiff is granted leave file the amended complaint substituting the Doe defendants only after obtaining this continuance.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Attendance At Deposition And Producing Documents And Imposing Sanctions Against East Bay Construction & Management Llc
Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion For Order Compelling Attendance At Deposition And Producing Documents And Imposing Sanctions Against East Bay Construction & Management LLC.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff 2075 Sutter Street Homeowners Association brings this unopposed motion for an order compelling defendant East Bay Construction & Management LLC to produce its PMQ for deposition accompanied by documents described in the deposition notice. (Rojas Dec., Ex. C.) The moving papers demonstrate that Plaintiff served the deposition notice on defendant, defendant did not serve objections, and although defendant's counsel engaged in some scheduling discussion did not agree to produce a deponent within a reasonable time of the noticed day and time. Although this is a closer call because Plaintiff's statement of facts concerned the document requests is somewhat cursory (see CCP 2025.450(a)(1)), the court is satisfied based on the entire declaration the accompanying request for production is supported by good cause.

Defendant is ordered to produce its PMQ deponent for deposition within 10 court days of notice of entry of this order.

Plaintiff also seeks monetary and evidentiary sanctions. The court finds that defendant did not act with substantial justification and has presented no circumstances making imposition of a sanction unjust. Accordingly, sanctions are mandatory. (CCP 2025.450(g)(1).) The court grants sanctions payable by defendant to plaintiff within 10 court days of notice of entry of this order in the amount of $2,500. The court does not order evidentiary sanctions at this time as it finds that compliance with its order will avoid prejudice to Plaintiff at trial. (See Manlin v. Milner (2022) 82 Cal.App.5th 1004, 1024 ["A trial court has broad discretion to impose discovery sanctions, but they should be appropriate to the dereliction, and should not exceed that which is required to protect the interests of the party entitled to but denied discovery. A discovery sanction should only be such as is suitable and necessary to enable the party seeking discovery to obtain the objects of the discovery he seeks, and may not be designed to impose punishment."] [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Liver Ramiro Guzman To Comply With Deposition Subpoena, To Appear For His Deposition And To Produce Subpoenaed Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-07-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION's Motion To Compel Liver Ramiro Guzman To Comply With Deposition Subpoena, To Appear For His Deposition And To Produce Subpoenaed Documents And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications to include the amount of sanctions.

Plaintiff 2075 Sutter Street HOA moves for an order compelling compliance with its deposition subpoena to Liver Ramiro Guzman and for sanctions. No opposition was filed. Plaintiff has shown that the deposition subpoena was properly served and that Guzman did not appear. Plaintiff has shown further that it attempted to contact Guzman to inquire about the nonappearance and that it served Guzman with its motion papers. For good cause shown in Plaintiff's moving papers, the court grants the motion. Guzman is ordered to attend and testify at a rescheduled deposition, and to produce for inspection any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice. The rescheduled deposition shall be noticed for a date within 15 days of notice of entry of this order, but Guzman must be given at least 10 days' notice of the rescheduled deposition. The Guzman and the parties to the litigation may mutually agree to extend the time for deposition for scheduling convenience.

Plaintiff seeks sanctions. CCP 2025.450(g)(1) provides, "If a motion under subdivision (a) is granted, the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . in favor of the party who noticed the deposition and against the deponent or the party with whom the deponent is affiliated, unless the court finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court finds Guzman has not acted with substantial justification. The court orders sanctions payable by Guzman to Plaintiff; sanctions shall be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Sanctions issued in the amount of $500 dollars.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
